svg.text: OpenTelemetry defines three signals: Traces, Logs, Metrics · tracing crate provides the Rust instrumentation API · ro11y bridges the gap

g.text: Tree of spans representing a request across services
· kind: Server | Client | Internal | Producer | Consumer
Rich field types: Display, Debug, i64, bool
No trace_id — spans are local only
Designed for diagnostics, not distributed tracing
✓ Assigns trace_id (BLAKE3 from CF-ID or UUID v4)
Logs bridge existing log systems to OTLP.
Not a replacement for application logging.
Events: trace!() debug!() info!() warn!() error!()
Level: TRACE / DEBUG / INFO / WARN / ERROR
Maps tracing events → OTLP log records
· UpDownCounter — can increase or decrease (queue depth)
· Async Counter, Async UpDownCounter, Async Gauge
· Exemplars: link a metric data point → trace_id
Most complex of the three signals — histogram encoding
alone requires boundaries[], bucket_counts[], sum, count, min, max
But there is no semantic meaning.
No counters. No gauges. No histograms.
converts these to actual metric types
OtlpLayer (tracing subscriber) → protobuf encoder → HTTP exporter
stderr JSON via ECS log driver
ECS Fargate · OTLP source → decode → route
traces → parquet codec → S3 sink → traces/
logs → parquet codec → S3 sink → logs/
metrics: log events with metric fields
log_to_metric transform available but adds complexity
Metric events live in logs/ (see below)
Metrics in Parquet — Current Approach vs Future
Current: Metrics as Log Events in logs/
Metric events are just log records with special fields.
They live in the logs/ Parquet table alongside regular logs.
Query metrics from the logs table:
FROM logs WHERE attributes['metric'] IS NOT NULL
Future: Dedicated Metrics Parquet (requires ro11y changes)
Vector receives native OTLP metrics, routes to dedicated table.
4. HTTP POST to /v1/metrics + Vector OTLP metrics pipeline
Current — zero additional code, metrics queryable from logs table, but no aggregation, histogram values are individual data points, value stored as string
Future — requires ~400 lines of protobuf encoding + instrument API, but gives proper aggregation, typed values, histogram buckets, standard OTLP wire format
Recommendation — current approach works for low-to-medium metric volume; invest in native OTLP metrics when cardinality or histogram accuracy becomes a bottleneck

svg.text: OTLP spec: opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/otlp · tracing: docs.rs/tracing · Vector: vector.dev
